etymology.description: phonetic 戶(戶) (hù, "door") + semantic 隹(隹) (zhuī, "bird")
etymology.interpretation: The character 雇 (gù, "hire") combines 戶(戶) (hù, "door") as a phonetic component with 隹(隹) (zhuī, "bird") as a semantic component, suggesting a context involving movement or transition, akin to employment or hiring.

examples.chinese: 公司决定雇一些新员工(公司決定雇一些新員工)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngsī juédìng gù yīxiē xīn yuángōng.
examples.english: The company decided to hire some new employees.

examples.chinese: 他们打算雇一位专业律师(他們打算雇一位專業律師)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen dǎsuàn gù yī wèi zhuānyè lǜshī.
examples.english: They plan to hire a professional lawyer.

examples.chinese: 老板正在考虑雇更多的兼职工人(老闆正在考慮雇更多的兼職工人)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎobǎn zhèngzài kǎolǜ gù gèng duō de jiānzhí gōngrén.
examples.english: The boss is considering hiring more part-time workers.
